card.intuition: The series of stages a React component goes through from the moment it's created and added to the page until it's removed.
card.analogy: A theatrical play: the actors arrive and get into costume (Mounting), they perform and react to the audience (Updating), and finally the curtain closes and they leave the stage (Unmounting).
when_to_use: Fetching data when a page loads (Mount)
Cleaning up interval timers or event listeners (Unmount)
Syncing state changes with third-party libraries (Update)
gotchas: In functional components, all these stages are handled by the `useEffect` hook
If you forget to 'clean up' in the unmount stage, you'll create memory leaks and 'state update on unmounted component' warnings

steps.annotation: Mount: component created. State initialised, constructor runs.
Render: React calls the function, producing a virtual DOM tree.
Commit: React diffs virtual DOM and patches the real DOM. useLayoutEffect fires.
useEffect runs after paint. On state/prop change, re-render begins.
Unmount: component removed. useEffect cleanup runs to cancel subscriptions/timers.

exercise.prompt: Match the lifecycle stage to the useEffect dependency array.
hints: An empty list means 'no dependencies', so it only runs once
Passing 'id' means it watches that specific variable
exercise.test_code:
assert "[]" in stages.values()
assert "id" in stages.values()
print("PASS")
